meaning: adj.俱乐部的,喜交际的,资格要求严的
examples: The pl ace has aw arm, clubby atmosphere.
No one could describe the atmosphere on HP's board as clubby.
IN THE clubby world of Korean commerce, hostile takeovers are rare.
He has built his empire as an outsider on the fringes of Mexico's clubby business community.
He also heads the clubby group of Toaster spotters who meet inside the cemetery.
